project.name: EdgeForms
project.description: Edge-native form builder SaaS API — Hono on Cloudflare Workers with Supabase backend

observability.distributedTracingNote: Deferred to v1.1 — single Cloudflare Worker deployment, CF-Ray-ID provides sufficient correlation for MVP. OTel will be added when multi-region edge routing is introduced.

assumptions: All API routes run on Cloudflare Workers — no traditional server provisioned. deploymentTarget is 'custom' because 'cloudflare-workers' is not in the standard enum.
Supabase RLS policies are the second line of defence — Hono middleware JWT validation is the first
Every Supabase table has RLS enabled with explicit workspace_id-based USING clauses — no table is public without a policy
Supabase service_role key is stored as a Cloudflare Worker secret — never exposed to client or committed to Git
CORS is enforced via an explicit ALLOWED_ORIGIN environment variable — wildcard is not permitted
All Stripe webhook events are verified with HMAC-SHA256 before processing — verification key stored in Cloudflare secret
Public form submission endpoint is rate-limited: 60 submissions/hour per IP via Cloudflare KV sliding window
IP addresses are stored as SHA-256 hashes (salted) — no raw IP is persisted
CSV export is asynchronous — triggered via Supabase Edge Function, result stored in private Supabase Storage bucket
API keys are hashed with bcrypt before storage — raw key is returned only once at creation
CF-Ray-ID is included as correlationId in every structured log entry
Distributed tracing deferred to v1.1 — single-region edge deployment, CF-Ray provides sufficient trace context for MVP
All Stripe PaymentIntent and subscription requests use idempotency keys derived from workspace_id + operation — retried payment calls cannot create duplicate charges.
External API calls (Stripe, Resend) are wrapped with AbortController timeout (10 seconds). Resend email failures use exponential backoff retry (3 attempts) and do not block the main request path.
wrangler.toml is committed at the repo root and defines Worker routes, KV namespace bindings, Cloudflare zone, and environment-specific deploy config.
